common_medicines.name: Metformin
common_medicines.dosage: As prescribed by doctor, typically 500-2000mg daily

common_medicines.name: Salbutamol
common_medicines.dosage: Inhaler: 1-2 puffs when needed. Max 8 puffs/day

do: Eat balanced meals with complex carbohydrates
health_tips.seek_care: Persistent high readings (>200 mg/dL), symptoms like excessive thirst, frequent urination, blurred vision, or wounds that don't heal
Severe headache, chest pain, vision problems, blood pressure above 180/120 mmHg
High fever with severe body aches, skin rash, bleeding from gums or nose, severe abdominal pain
Chest pain or pressure, shortness of breath, pain in arm/jaw/neck, dizziness, cold sweats
Persistent sadness lasting more than 2 weeks, thoughts of self-harm, severe anxiety, sleep problems, changes in appetite

do: Eat 5 portions of fruits and vegetables daily
health_tips.seek_care: Unexplained weight loss/gain, digestive problems, food allergies, eating disorders
Blood in urine, reduced urine output, swelling in legs/feet, persistent back pain, fatigue

do: Eat foods rich in Vitamin A
health_tips.seek_care: Sudden vision changes, eye pain, seeing flashes or floaters, redness with discharge

do: Stay active to improve lung capacity
health_tips.seek_care: Persistent cough lasting more than 3 weeks, difficulty breathing, coughing blood, chest pain while breathing

do: Take folic acid if planning pregnancy
health_tips.seek_care: Abnormal bleeding, breast lumps, severe menstrual pain, missed periods, pelvic pain
High fever in infants, poor feeding, developmental concerns, persistent diarrhea, breathing difficulty
Sudden confusion, falls, unexplained weight loss, persistent pain, vision/hearing changes

lab_ranges.note: Measure at rest, multiple readings recommended
Lower is better for heart health

lab_ranges.high_indication: Low HDL increases heart disease risk
lab_ranges.note: Higher is better for heart health
Affected by diet and kidney function
Common deficiency in Sri Lanka despite sun

common_conditions.when_to_seek_help: Symptoms lasting more than 2 weeks, thoughts of self-harm
When anxiety interferes with daily life
When stress is overwhelming or persistent

self_help_tips: Take breaks from screens and news

vaccination_campaigns.name: Flu Vaccination Campaign
vaccination_campaigns.eligibility: Elderly (60+), healthcare workers, chronic disease patients

complaints.acknowledgement: Complaint registered. Ticket ID: C-90321. We will forward this to the concerned team.

pregnancy_childcare.detail: First visit: Before 12 weeks. Monthly visits until 28 weeks, fortnightly until 36 weeks, then weekly until delivery.
Seek immediate care for: severe headache, vision changes, sudden swelling, vaginal bleeding, reduced baby movement, severe abdominal pain, or water breaking.
Exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months, followed by introduction of complementary foods. Follow vaccination schedule strictly.
Track growth, speech, and motor skills with clinic guidance. Each child develops at their own pace, but significant delays should be evaluated.
Balanced diet with all food groups. Limit sugar and processed foods. Encourage fruits, vegetables, and adequate protein.

alerts.title: Dengue Alert
alerts.guidance: Remove stagnant water, use mosquito nets and repellents. Report fever cases early.

alerts.title: Heatwave Advisory
alerts.guidance: Stay hydrated, avoid midday sun exposure, check on elderly and children.

alerts.title: Flu Season Advisory
alerts.guidance: Practice hand hygiene, consider flu vaccination, stay home if symptomatic.

ambulance_details.emergency_note: In medical emergencies, immediately call the official government ambulance service.

services.dispatch_model: Centralized national dispatch with regional units
usage_instructions: Stay calm and provide clear location
services.data_source: Ministry of Health / National Emergency Ambulance Service

vaccination_programs.program_name: National Expanded Immunization Schedule - Routine Childhood Vaccines
vaccination_programs.description: Routine immunization under the National Immunization Programme to prevent vaccine-preventable diseases.

schedule.notes: Given before leaving hospital, preferably within 24 hrs of birth.

vaccination_programs.notes: Part of the National Immunization Programme. Timing based on national guidelines.

vaccination_programs.description: National COVID-19 vaccination program for protection against SARS-CoV-2.
vaccination_programs.notes: Booster doses available for eligible populations. Check MOH guidelines for current recommendations.

vaccination_programs.description: Periodic polio vaccination activities to maintain eradication status.
vaccination_programs.notes: Sri Lanka has maintained polio-free status. Supplementary campaigns during outbreak risk periods.

vaccination_programs.description: Annual flu vaccination for high-risk groups.
vaccination_programs.target_population: Elderly (60+), healthcare workers, chronic disease patients, pregnant women
vaccination_programs.availability: MOH offices, government hospitals, select private hospitals
vaccination_programs.notes: Recommended annually as flu strains change each year.

vaccination_programs.description: HPV vaccine information for cervical cancer prevention.
vaccination_programs.notes: Available at select facilities. Consult healthcare provider for eligibility and schedule.

symptoms: Pain in arm, jaw, or back
immediate_action: Have person sit or lie down comfortably
Give aspirin if not allergic (chew one 325mg tablet)
Be ready to perform CPR if needed
Do not give food, water, or medicine
do_not: Let them fall asleep without monitoring

immediate_action: Make a fist above their navel
do_not: Slap the back while standing (do if person is bending forward)

immediate_action: Apply firm pressure with clean cloth
Elevate wound above heart level if possible
Keep pressure for at least 10 minutes
Add more cloth if blood soaks through (don't remove first)
do_not: Release pressure to check wound frequently

immediate_action: Cool with running water for 10-20 minutes
Seek medical help for serious burns
Stay with them until fully recovered

tips: Understand your policy coverage before treatment
Keep all medical bills and prescriptions

ayurveda.description: Traditional Sri Lankan/Indian medicine system using natural herbs and treatments

traditional_medicine.note: Traditional medicine should complement, not replace, modern medical treatment for serious conditions. Always inform your doctor about any traditional treatments you're using.
